title: Filter module for reducing differential and common mode noise and method to manufacture such a filter module
abstract: A filter module (1) for reducing differential and common mode electrical noise comprising: two busbars (2,3), an at least partially electrically conductive housing (4),a two common mode choke (5,6) arranged in the housing (4), the common mode chokes (5,6) being formed by ferrite cores (7,8), at least a first bypass capacitor (9) connected to the busbars (2,3), at least a second bypass capacitor (10) connected to the first busbar (2) and a midpoint (11), at least a third bypass capacitor (12) connected to the second busbar (3) and the midpoint (11), the first bypass capacitor (9), the second bypass capacitor (10) and the third bypass capacitor (12) being arranged on a circuit board (13), the circuit board (13) being arranged in the housing (4) between the ferrite cores (7,8) and having first spring contacts (14,15) providing an electrical connection between the circuit board (13) and the busbar (2, 3).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A filter module (1 ) for reducing differential and common mode electrical noise comprising: - at least a first electrically conductive busbar (2) and a second electrically con ductive busbar (3) being spaced apart, - an at least partially electrically conductive housing (4) at least partially enclos ing the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3), - at least a first common mode choke (5) and a second common mode choke (6) arranged in the housing (4) and being spaced apart each other, - the first common mode choke (5) being formed by a first ferrite core (7) through which the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) extend, - the second common mode choke (6) being formed by a second ferrite core (8) through which the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) extend, - at least a first bypass capacitor (9) which is electrically connected to the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3), - at least a second bypass capacitor (10) which is electrically connected to the first busbar (2) and a midpoint (11 ), - at least a third bypass capacitor (12) which is electrically connected to the second busbar (3) and the midpoint (11 ), - said midpoint (11 ) being electrically connected to the housing (4), - the first bypass capacitor (9), the second bypass capacitor (10) and the third bypass capacitor (12) being arranged on a circuit board (13), - the circuit board (13) being arranged in the housing (4) between the first ferrite core (7) and the second ferrite core (8), 

 - the circuit board (13) having at least a first spring contact (14) and a second spring contact (15), - the first spring contact (14) providing an electrical connection between the circuit board (13) and the first busbar (2), - the second spring contact (15) providing an electrical connection between the circuit board (13) and the second busbar (3). 2. The filter module (1 ) according to claim 1 , wherein the first busbar (2) and/or the second busbar (3) have at least partially an insulating sheath (16). 3. The filter module (1 ) according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the first ferrite core (7) has a toroidal shape and/or the second ferrite core (8) has a toroidal shape. 4. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first ferrite core (7) is made of one piece and/or the second ferrite core (8) is made of one piece. 5. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first spring contact (14) comprises at least two spring fingers (17, 17a) and/or the second spring contact (15) comprises at least two spring fingers (18, 18a). 6. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first bypass capacitor (9) is a class-X capacitor while the the second bypass capacitor (10) and the third bypass capacitor (12) are class-Y capacitors. 7. The filter module (1 ) according to any of claims 2 to 6, 

 wherein a at least a part of the insulating sheath (16) of the first busbar (2) and/or the second busbar (3) forms a mounting for the first ferrite core (7) and/or for the second ferrite core (8). 8. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising - at least a fourth bypass capacitor (19) which is electrically connected to the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3), - at least a fifth bypass capacitor (20) which is electrically connected to the first busbar (2) and the midpoint (11 ), - at least a sixth bypass capacitor (21 ) which is electrically connected to the second busbar (3) and the midpoint (11 ), - the fourth bypass capacitor (19), the fifth bypass capacitor (20) and the sixth bypass capacitor (21 ) being arranged on the circuit board (13). 9. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) divide the circuit board (13) in a first part (22) and a second part (23). 10. The filter module (1 ) according to claim 9 and 8, wherein the first bypass capacitor (9), the second bypass capacitor (10) and the third bypass capacitor (12) are arranged on the first part (22) of the circuit board (13) while the the fourth bypass capacitor (19), the fifth bypass capacitor (20) and the sixth bypass capacitor (21 ) are arranged on the second part (23) of the circuit board (13). 11. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, - wherein the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) each are spaced apart from the circuit board (13), and/or 

 - wherein the first spring contact (14) forms an electrical connection between the circuit board (13) and the first busbar (2), while the second spring contact (15) forms an electrical connection between the circuit board (13) and the second busbar (3). 12. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the surface of the first busbar (2) facing the circuit board (13) is mini mized and wherein the surface of the second busbar (3) facing the circuit board (13) is minimized. 13. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first spring contact (14) is electrically connected to a conductor track and/or electrical line of the circuit board (13), and wherein the second spring con tact (15) is electrically connected to a conductor track and/or electrical line of the circuit board (13). 14. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) are facing the same side of the circuit board (13), and/or wherein the first spring contact (14) and the second spring contact (15) are arranged on the same side of the circuit board (13). 15. The filter module (1 ) according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the first spring contact (14) and the second spring contact (15) each are formed from electrically conductive material. 16. A method to manufacture a filter module (1 ) according to any of claims 1 to 10 or claims 11 to 15, the method comprising the following steps: - providing an at least partially electrically conductive housing (4) with at least a first portion (24) and a second portion (25), 

 - providing at least a first electrically conductive busbar and a second electrical ly conductive busbar with at least two ferrite cores, - the first busbar (2), the second busbar (3) and ferrite cores are placed in the first portion (24) of the housing (4). 17. The method according to claim 16, the method further comprising the follow ing steps: - providing a circuit board (13) which is preassembled with at least one bypass capacitor, a first spring contact (14) and a second spring contact (15), - placing the circuit board (13) between the ferrite cores, such that the first bus bar (2) and second busbar (3) are at least partially arranged between the cir cuit board (13) and the first portion (24) of the housing (4), - the first spring contact (14) and the second spring contact (15) electrically contacting the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3), respectively, - attaching the circuit board (13) to the first portion (24) of the housing (4). 18. The method according to claim 17, wherein the circuit board (13) is attached to the first portion (24) of the housing (4) by at least one screw. 19. The method according to claim 18, wherein the electrical contact between the midpoint (11 ) of the circuit board (13) and the housing (4) is adjusted by tightening the screw. 20. The method according to any of claims 16 to 19, wherein the second portion (25) of the housing (4) is attached to the first portion (24) of the housing (4). 21. The method according to any of claims 17 to 20, 

 wherein the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) are connected to the first spring contact (14) and to the second spring contact (15), respectively, by moving the preassembled circuit board (13) towards the first busbar (2) and the second busbar (3) along a direction perpendicular to the circuit board (13) surface on which the first spring contact (14) and the second spring contact (15) are preas sembled. 22. The method according to any of claims 17 to 21 , wherein the first spring contact (14) and the second spring contact (15) are sol dered onto the circuit board (13) in the same process step in which the other component are soldered onto the circuit board (13).
